ai_summary: Bank of India has received an 'IND AA+/Stable' Issuer Rating and had its Basel III Tier 2 and Infrastructure Bonds reaffirmed at the same rating by India Ratings & Research, reflecting strong systemic importance, improved capital, and asset quality. While generally positive, a key area of concern is the bank's "pressured deposit profile," indicated by lagging deposit growth, increasing reliance on bulk funds, and a rising loan-to-deposit ratio (82%), which could constrain future growth and liquidity. This deposit challenge is a material impact that warrants close monitoring despite the overall positive rating.
